extra.message: Hardcoded cryptographic secrets: SECRET_KEY_HMAC='secret', SECRET_KEY_HMAC_2, and Flask secret_key are hardcoded in source code. Attackers gaining source access can forge JWTs and session cookies.
Hardcoded admin credentials: username='admin', password='admin123' are seeded into the database. Stored plaintext password enables trivial account compromise.

insecure_verify() decodes a JWT token with verify=False, completely disabling signature verification. Any attacker-supplied token is accepted. Used in /get/<cust_id> endpoint allowing authentication bypass.

The 404 error handler interpolates request.url directly into a template string that is rendered with render_template_string, exposing dir/help/locals. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) leading to remote code execution via crafted URLs.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: Passwords are hashed with MD5, which is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage. No salt is used. Attackers can trivially crack credentials.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: /register/user endpoint has no authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can create admin-level User accounts.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The login endpoint queries User by username AND plaintext password without hashing the submitted password (even though /register/user hashes incoming passwords with MD5). Credentials are stored/compared inconsistently; password is compared in plaintext against DB values.

/fetch/customer retrieves any customer record by supplied id without verifying the JWT user is authorized to access that record. Returns sensitive fields including credit card number (ccn). Insecure Direct Object Reference.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: /get/<cust_id> exposes any customer record (including cc_num) based on URL path id with no ownership check, and uses insecure_verify() which disables JWT signature validation. Combined IDOR and auth bypass.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Customer credit card numbers (ccn) are returned in API JSON responses from /fetch/customer and /get/<cust_id>. Sensitive financial data is exposed to any authenticated request.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: User-supplied 'search' value from request.json is formatted directly into a SQL string via Python % interpolation and executed with db.engine.execute(). This is classic SQL injection.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

extra.message: Exception message (which can contain attacker-controlled SQL input) is interpolated into an HTML string rendered with render_template_string and dir/help/locals exposed. Allows Server-Side Template Injection and XSS.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: /xxe_uploader accepts arbitrary uploaded file and parses it as a DOCX via python-docx (which uses lxml). Unauthenticated endpoint, no content type validation — exploitable for XML External Entity attacks on crafted docx XML parts.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: /xxe_uploader file upload endpoint has no authentication and accepts arbitrary files saved to the /Files/ directory.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: yaml.load() called on user-uploaded file without SafeLoader. PyYAML default loader supports arbitrary Python object instantiation via tags such as !!python/object/apply:os.system, enabling remote code execution.
/yaml_hammer endpoint accepts file uploads and parses YAML without any authentication check.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Exception details (e.message) are returned in the JSON error response, leaking internal information such as stack traces or DB errors to clients.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: Content extracted from uploaded DOCX paragraphs is rendered to a template via 'name=para.text'. If the template marks this as safe or uses |safe, attacker-controlled document content could produce reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
